training_directory: Directory where experiment outputs and training artefacts are stored.
enable_web_interface: Enable or disable the web UI.
server_user: Unix user used by the runtime when launching Mininet-related processes.
log_level: Logging verbosity used by the application.
random_seed: Seed used to make runs reproducible.
env_params.gym_type:
Select the scenario pipeline to run. Variants ending in _from_dataset replay pre-recorded statuses JSON files instead of generating live/synthetic traffic.
  • marl_pz — Multi-agent PettingZoo parallel API. Host agents train simultaneously; each observes only its own host (SingleAgentView). Supports coordinator communication strategies.
  • marl_pz_from_dataset — Same as marl_pz but replays pre-recorded traffic data.
  • marl_attacks — Multi-agent attacks, sequential training. Each host agent trains one at a time in its own attack/defend cycle.
  • marl_attacks_from_dataset — Same as marl_attacks from pre-recorded data.
  • classification — Parallel single-agent classification. One agent per host, all run simultaneously; classifies traffic as normal or anomalous.
  • classification_from_dataset — Same as classification from pre-recorded data.
  • attacks — Single global agent monitors the entire network for attacks; host agents run in parallel.
  • attacks_from_dataset — Same as attacks from pre-recorded data.
  • attacks_ho — Host-Observable single agent. One independent agent per host trained sequentially (like MARL); each agent observes only its own host traffic.
  • attacks_ho_from_dataset — Same as attacks_ho from pre-recorded data.
env_params.show_normal_traffic: Show normal traffic generation in the logs and UI.
env_params.must_check_env: Enable extra environment checks before running.
env_params.n_bins: Number of bins used by state discretization. Higher values preserve more detail but increase state-space size for tabular agents.
env_params.steps_min_percentage: Minimum fraction of episode steps required before early-stop checks are considered valid. Typical range: 0.7-0.95.
env_params.accuracy_min: Accuracy threshold used by early-stop logic. If reached (with steps_min_percentage satisfied), training can terminate before exhausting episodes.
env_params.max_steps: Maximum number of steps per episode.
ui.scenario_source_help:
Scenario source behavior:

Generate new (default): a new scenario is created when training starts.
Generate test scenario: creates a preview in memory and opens the analysis popup; no file is saved.
Load existing: click a row to select an existing scenario.json and open its popup details.

When entering Training with Load existing:
- episodes <- scenario training episodes
- max_steps <- scenario train max steps
- test_episodes <- scenario eval episodes
This sync happens only when navigating to Training, not during inspection.
env_params.attacks: Attack scheduling and mitigation parameters for ATTACKS/ATTACKS_HO/MARL attack-oriented scenarios.
env_params.attacks.likely: Base probability that a host starts an attack when idle. This value can dynamically evolve during generation depending on scenario logic.
env_params.attacks.likely_train: Attack likelihood during TRAINING episodes (ATTACKS_HO only). Range: 0.0-1.0, default 0.9. Higher values create frequent attacks for agent learning and policy development.
env_params.attacks.likely_eval: Attack likelihood during EVALUATION/TEST episodes (ATTACKS_HO only). Range: 0.0-1.0, default 0.3. Lower values provide realistic sparse attack conditions for final testing.
env_params.attacks.max_attack_percentage: Upper cap for effective attack probability (0.0-1.0). Example: 0.9 means attack likelihood can scale up to 90% in high-pressure phases.
env_params.attacks.short_attack_duration: Duration (steps) of SHORT_ATTACK windows. Lower values create bursty spikes; higher values create sustained micro-attacks.
env_params.attacks.long_attack_duration: Duration (steps) of LONG_ATTACK windows. Increase for persistent attacks; reduce for more fragmented behavior.
env_params.attacks.no_attack_timeout: Cooldown (steps) after an attack before the same host can attack again. Lower values increase attack density.
env_params.attacks.unblock_min_hold_roun: Minimum full rounds to keep a host blocked before allowing unblock.
env_params.attacks.unblock_required_norm: Consecutive NORMAL decisions required while blocked before unblocking.
env_params.attacks.apply_drop_rules: If true, the environment applies drop rules to the traffic when the agent takes the 'drop' action. This can be used to simulate the effect of blocking traffic in a real network. If false, the environment will not apply drop rules and will only provide feedback to the agent based on its actions.
env_params.attacks.include_percentage_va: ATTACKS_HO only. Controls the per-host observation size. false (default) → shape (4,): RX packets, RX bytes, TX packets, TX bytes — counters only, less noise, faster convergence. true → shape (8,): adds 4 percentage-variation columns (Δ%). Changing this value makes saved models incompatible.
env_params.attacks.thresholds: Thresholds used by rule-based labeling/detection features in attack environments.
env_params.attacks.thresholds.packets: Absolute packet-count threshold. Values above this are treated as anomalous/suspicious depending on scenario logic.
env_params.attacks.thresholds.var_packet: Allowed packet variation (%) versus baseline. Above this delta, traffic may be flagged as unstable/anomalous.
env_params.attacks.thresholds.bytes: Absolute byte-volume threshold used with packet thresholds to characterize attack intensity.
env_params.attacks.thresholds.var_bytes: Allowed byte variation (%) versus baseline. Useful for volumetric anomaly detection.
env_params.classification.thresholds: Thresholds used by classification scenarios to map traffic statistics into labels.
env_params.attacks.use_coordinator: Include a coordinator agent that aggregates host signals and broadcasts alerts back to all hosts. When true, host observations gain one extra feature (msg_count). Superseded by the communication.strategy field — if a communication: section is present, use_coordinator is derived automatically from strategy (none → false, any other → true).
env_params.communication: Inter-agent communication strategy for marl_pz scenarios. Defines how host agents share attack evidence with the coordinator and with each other. If this section is absent, behaviour falls back to attacks.use_coordinator.
env_params.communication.strategy:
Communication protocol between host agents and the coordinator.
  • none — agents act independently, no coordinator in the environment, host observations have no msg_count feature.
  • naive_broadcast (S0) — every host reports its action to the coordinator; the coordinator relays a binary alert to all hosts. All agent types.
  • uaq (S1) — Uncertainty-Aware Querying: hosts compute their policy entropy H(π) and flag alerts as uncertain (H > tau) or confident (H ≤ tau_expert). Tabular full support; DQN/PPO/A2C treated as always-uncertain.
  • federated_sync (S2) — Tabular and DQN: Q-table/weight averaging every sync_interval steps (both use the joint per-step training loop). PPO/A2C: FedAvg weight averaging every sync_interval_episodes episodes (episode-interleaved loop, no replay buffer to step jointly).
  • policy_exchange (S3) — Tabular and DQN: copy best Q-table/policy weights to lagging agents after each episode (joint per-step loop). PPO/A2C: same copy rule, but via the episode-interleaved loop.
  • experience_sharing (S4) — Tabular: broadcast high-|TD-error| transitions between Q-tables every exchange_interval steps (joint loop). DQN: share high-|reward| transitions between replay buffers every exchange_interval steps (joint loop). PPO/A2C: not supported (on-policy — no replay buffer); a warning is logged and the strategy is ignored for those agents.
  • hierarchical (S5) — cluster-head aggregation for large IoT topologies (NOT IMPLEMENTED YET).
env_params.communication.uaq: Parameters for the UAQ strategy (S1 — Uncertainty-Aware Querying). Only active when strategy is set to 'uaq'.
env_params.communication.uaq.tau: Entropy threshold above which a host is considered uncertain. Uses normalised Shannon entropy H(π)/log(n_actions) ∈ [0, 1], where 1.0 = all actions equally likely (max uncertainty) and 0.0 = fully decided. Hosts with entropy > tau flag their alert as 'uncertain' (message value 1), which the coordinator ignores. Default 0.8. Lower values make the filter stricter — fewer hosts qualify as uncertain.
env_params.communication.uaq.tau_expert: Entropy threshold below which a host is considered a confident expert. Hosts with entropy ≤ tau_expert send a 'confident attack' signal (message value 2) that the coordinator counts toward its consensus. Must be < tau. Default 0.3. Lower values require stronger certainty before a host is trusted. Hosts with tau_expert < entropy ≤ tau (moderate confidence) send no message at all — not trusted as confident, not uncertain enough to flag.
env_params.communication.uaq.cooldown_st: Reserved for future per-host query-rate limiting (not yet active). Will cap how often a single host can send messages per N steps.
env_params.communication.federated_sync: Parameters for the Federated Policy Sync strategy (S2). Active only when strategy is set to 'federated_sync'. Supported by tabular agents and DQN (via the joint per-step loop); PPO/A2C use a slower episode-interleaved loop instead.
Tabular and DQN: number of environment steps between averaging rounds (both use the joint per-step training loop). PPO/A2C: acts as sync_interval_episodes instead (averaging rounds per episode), since they have no per-step hook to gate on. Lower values = more frequent synchronisation. Default 100 steps (tabular/DQN) / 1 episode (PPO/A2C).
env_params.communication.policy_exchange: Parameters for the Policy Exchange strategy (S3). Active only when strategy is set to 'policy_exchange'. Supported by all agent types (tabular, DQN, PPO, A2C).

Minimum episode reward gap between the best-performing agent and a peer to trigger a policy copy. If best_reward - peer_reward > lag_threshold, the peer's Q-table is overwritten with the best agent's Q-table. Prevents unnecessary copying when performance is similar. Default 10.0.
env_params.communication.experience_shar: Parameters for the Experience Replay Sharing strategy (S4). Active only when strategy is set to 'experience_sharing'. Tabular agents and DQN supported. PPO/A2C not supported (on-policy — no replay buffer); a warning is logged and those agents are excluded.
Number of environment steps between experience broadcast rounds. Every exchange_interval steps, each agent broadcasts its highest-TD-error transitions to peers. Default 50.

Minimum value for a transition to be shared. Tabular: interpreted as |TD-error| = |r + γ·maxQ(s') − Q(s,a)|. DQN: interpreted as |reward| (used as a proxy — network forward pass avoided for efficiency). Transitions below this threshold are discarded. Default 1.0.
Maximum number of transitions shared per agent per broadcast round. The most recent qualifying transitions are used. Default 50.
env_params.pettingzoo: PettingZoo Parallel API parameters. These settings are specific to marl_pz scenarios and control reward sharing and episode length at the PZ level.
env_params.pettingzoo.local_ratio: Balance between individual and shared team reward. Range [0.0, 1.0]. 1.0 = fully individual: each agent is rewarded only for its own traffic segment. 0.0 = fully cooperative: all agents receive the mean team reward. 0.5 = equal mix. Default 1.0. Lower values encourage cooperation but can slow per-agent convergence.
env_params.pettingzoo.shared_reward: If true, overrides local_ratio and gives all agents the exact same mean team reward every step, regardless of individual performance. Useful for fully cooperative setups. Default false.
env_params.pettingzoo.max_cycles: PettingZoo name for episode length. If set to a number, overrides env_params.max_steps for this scenario. Leave as null to use max_steps.
env_params.pettingzoo.render_mode: PettingZoo standard render mode. null = no rendering (default for headless training). 'human' = print/display output. 'rgb_array' = return an image array from render().
env_params.net_params: Network topology and SDN controller configuration used to build the Mininet environment.
env_params.net_params.start_cli: Open the Mininet CLI after creating the network.
env_params.net_params.traffic_types: Allowed normal-traffic generators. Common set: none, ping, udp, tcp.
env_params.net_params.num_hosts: Number of hosts in the simulated network.
env_params.net_params.num_switches: Number of switches in the simulated network.
env_params.net_params.num_iot: Number of IoT nodes in the simulated network.
env_params.net_params.controller: OpenDayLight controller connection details. Ensure IP/port and credentials match your controller instance.
agents: List of agent configurations. Multiple enabled agents are trained/evaluated in the same run according to scenario mode.
agents.*.name: Display name of the agent instance.
agents.*.progress_bar: Show a progress bar while training this agent.
agents.*.skip_learn: Skip learning and use the agent only for loading or evaluation.
agents.*.show_action: Show the chosen action in the logs.
agents.*.load: If true, attempt to load model weights/artifacts before training/evaluation.
agents.*.load_dir: Relative path (from training root) to a saved run/model folder. Must contain artifacts compatible with the selected algorithm.
agents.*.save: Save the trained model at the end of the run.
agents.*.state_input_mode: Input representation for deep agents: normalized (recommended) or raw. Keep consistent with how the model was trained when loading checkpoints.
agents.*.net_arch: Neural network architecture for deep agents.
agents.*.discount_factor: Discount factor used by tabular agents.
agents.*.gamma: Discount factor used by deep agents.
agents.*.exploration_rate: Initial exploration rate for tabular agents.
agents.*.exploration_decay: Exploration decay applied after each episode.
agents.*.exploration_fraction: Fraction of training used for exploration in DQN.
agents.*.exploration_initial_eps: Initial epsilon value for DQN exploration.
agents.*.exploration_final_eps: Final epsilon value for DQN exploration.
agents.*.buffer_size: Replay buffer size used by DQN.
agents.*.batch_size: Mini-batch size used for gradient updates. Gold rule: must be batch_size less than n_steps * n_envs.
agents.*.target_update_interval: How often to update the DQN target network.
agents.*.learning_starts: Number of steps collected before learning starts.
agents.*.n_steps: Number of rollout steps used by PPO/A2C.
